compiled_state.title: validate-issues.sh fails with OSError 75 (Value too large for defined data type) at line 36 of embedded Python
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Discovered during brainstorm flow execution for epic 36a7-dbb5. Steps to reproduce:

1. Run: .claude/scripts/dso validate-issues.sh --quick --terse
2. Observe: Traceback (most recent call last): File <string>, line 36, in <module>; OSError: [Errno 75] Value too large for defined data type

This blocks Phase 3 Step 3 (Validate Ticket Health) of the brainstorm skill, but does not appear to affect the underlying ticket store (.claude/scripts/dso ticket show works correctly). Likely a size-overflow when the script enumerates the full ticket set.

The brainstorm for epic 36a7-dbb5 proceeded past this gate because the ticket itself was created and confirmed healthy via ticket show; the validate-issues failure is infrastructure-side only.

comments.body: Environment note: this OSError 75 was encountered in the Claude Code **web** environment (claude.ai/code), not a local install. The errno (EOVERFLOW — 'Value too large for defined data type') is characteristic of 32-bit/64-bit interface mismatches in containerized sandbox filesystems and may not reproduce in a standard local install. Reproduction in other environments (local CLI, IDE extension) should be verified before scoping the fix.
comments.author: Claude
